name: Do I need a permit to remodel my bathroom in Daly City or Pacifica?
headline: Do I need a permit to remodel my bathroom in Daly City or Pacifica?

mainEntity.name: Do I need a permit to remodel my bathroom in Daly City or Pacifica?
mainEntity.text: Do I need a permit to remodel my bathroom in Daly City or Pacifica?

acceptedAnswer.text: In most Bay Area cities like Daly City, Pacifica, San Bruno, and Hillsborough, you **do need a permit** for a bathroom remodel if you are changing plumbing, electrical, ventilation, or moving walls. Simple cosmetic work like painting or swapping out a faucet in the same location may not need one, but anything more than that usually does. Your local building department wants permits when you: - Relocate or add plumbing fixtures - Update wiring, add outlets, or change lighting - Replace a tub with a shower - Move or remove walls - Add or change ventilation fans Permits protect you by making sure the work meets safety codes. They also help when you sell your home; unpermitted work can show up in inspections and cause trouble in escrow. A common pitfall is assuming the contractor will "handle everything" without checking. Always ask who is pulling the permit and whose name it will be under. Before starting, call or check the website of your city’s building department for bathroom remodel rules. Ask your contractor to explain what inspections are required (often plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, and final). Skipping permits might seem cheaper or faster, but if the city finds out, you could face fines or be forced to open the walls again to prove the work is safe.
